description: A postsecondary psychology instructor has collected Müller-Lyer illusion data from 15 students (P01-P15) using a method-of-constant-stimuli paradigm. Unfortunately, a 16th participant (P99) had a stuck response key and their data is contaminated. The dataset is located at ~/pebl/data/mullerlyer_data.csv. Your task is to: 1) Identify and exclude the corrupted participant (P99) who responded 'comparison longer' on 100% of trials across all comparison lengths. 2) For each of the 15 valid participants, fit logistic psychometric functions to the proportion of 'comparison longer' responses (response=1) as a function of comparison_length for both the 'fins_in' and 'fins_out' conditions. 3) Estimate the Point of Subjective Equality (PSE) for each condition per participant (the comparison length where P('longer') = 0.5). 4) Compute the illusion magnitude for each participant (PSE_fins_in - PSE_fins_out). 5) Compute the group mean for PSE_fins_out, PSE_fins_in, and illusion_magnitude across all 15 valid participants. Save your results as a JSON file to ~/pebl/analysis/mullerlyer_report.json following this structure: {"participants": [{"id": "P01", "pse_fins_out": 88.3, "pse_fins_in": 112.1, "illusion_magnitude": 23.8}, ..., {"id": "P99", "excluded": true, "reason": "..."}], "group_mean_pse_fins_out": 89.6, "group_mean_pse_fins_in": 111.1, "group_mean_illusion_magnitude": 21.5}

metadata.data_source: Simulated empirical psychophysical data based on Predebon (2004) parameters
